groups.title: PUBLIC LIBRARY
groups.desc:
Questions 1-10
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Example The library re-opened last month The library now has • a seating area with magazines • an expanded section for books on $873$ • a new section on local $874$ • a community room for meetings (also possible to $875$ there) • a new section of books for $876$ For younger children • the next Science Club meeting: experiments using things from your $877$ • Reading Challenge: read six books during the holidays For adults • this Friday: a local author talks about a novel based on a real $878$ • IT support is available on Tuesdays - no $879$ is necessary • free check of blood $880$ and cholesterol levels (over 60s only) Other information • the library shop sells wall-charts, cards and $881$ • evenings and weekends: free $882$ is available

questions.title: · an expanded section for books on 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写"expanded section for books on ___"，而原文中明确提到了"a much larger section of the library devoted to travel books"。这里的"much larger section"对应了题目中的"expanded section"，而"travel books"则直接指明了这个扩大的区域是用于旅行类书籍的。因此，"travel"是最合适的答案。此外，原文中使用"devoted to"这个短语，进一步强调了这个区域专门用于旅行类书籍，这与题目中"for books on"的表述相符。作为考生，你需要注意听力中的同义词替换（expanded = much larger）以及关键信息的准确提取。

questions.title: · a new section on local 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写有关"local"（当地）的新部分，而原文中明确提到了一个新的部分（new section）包含了城镇（town）和周边地区（surrounding region）的历史（history）材料。"history"是最合适的答案，因为它既符合题目要求的"local"（当地的）概念，又是这个新部分的主要内容。此外，"history"是一个单词，符合题目要求填写一个单词的条件。虽然原文中没有直接使用"local"这个词，但"town and surrounding region"实际上就是在描述本地区域，因此"history"成为了最佳答案。

questions.title: · a community room for meetings (also possible to 【   】 there)
questions.analysis:
原因如下：
        1. 题目要求填写一个单词，描述在社区室除了开会之外还可以做什么。
        2. 答案句中明确提到，除了被租用来开会外，人们还可以"use it to study"（用它来学习）。
        3. "study"是一个动词，符合题目要求填写的词性。
        4. "study"与题目中的"meetings"形成对比，表明了社区室的两种不同用途。
        5. 这个答案准确反映了社区室在非会议时间的用途，与题目要求完全吻合。

        记住，在做这类填空题时，要注意听句子的转折部分（如这里的"but"），因为重要信息往往在转折之后。同时，要理解整个句子的结构，特别是"use it to + verb"这样的表达方式，它往往直接指向所需的答案。

questions.title: · a new section of books for 【   】
questions.analysis: 它直接对应了题干中要求的"新的书籍部分"。原文中明确提到了图书馆"引入"（introduced）了一个"特别的部分"（special section），专门为青少年（teenagers）提供小说。这个新增的部分正是题目所询问的内容。而且，"teenagers"作为一个特定的年龄群体，与题干中的"new section"完美对应，进一步确认了它是正确答案。其他提到的年龄组，如儿童，都是在讨论已有的服务或活动，而不是新增的书籍部分。因此，"teenagers"是唯一符合题目要求的答案。

questions.title: · the next Science Club meeting: experiments using things from your 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写科学俱乐部下次会议将用来做实验的物品来源。原文中明确提到他们将使用"stuff that everyone has in the kitchen"（每个人厨房里都有的东西）来做实验。虽然原文举例提到了糖和面粉，但题目要求的是这些物品的来源地，即"kitchen"（厨房）。这个答案不仅直接对应了题目要求，还体现了考生需要从具体例子中提取关键信息的能力。

questions.title: · this Friday: a local author talks about a novel based on a real 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写本地作家的小说所基于的内容。在原文中，我们可以清楚地听到"she based the story on a crime that actually took place here years ago"（她是根据多年前这里实际发生的一起犯罪事件写成的故事）。这里的"crime"（犯罪）就是小说的基础，也就是题目所要求的答案。这个词完美地符合题目的要求，因为它既是一个单词，又准确描述了小说的基础内容。此外，"actually took place"（实际发生）这个短语进一步确认了这是一个真实事件，而不仅仅是虚构的故事。

questions.title: · IT support is available on Tuesdays - no  【   】 is necessary
questions.analysis: 原文中明确提到了关于IT支持服务的信息。原文说"It's on Tuesday mornings - they don't need to make an appointment or anything, they just turn up."这句话直接表明参加IT支持服务不需要预约（appointment）。题目中要求填写的正是"no [ ] is necessary"中的空白，而"appointment"正好符合这个语境。此外，原文中的"they just turn up"（他们只需直接来）进一步强调了不需要预约的概念。因此，"appointment"是最合适的答案。

questions.title: · free check of blood  【   】 and cholesterol levels (over 60s only)
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写的是与"blood"相关的检查项目。在原文中，提到了医院安排人来测量血液中的"sugar"（糖分）水平。虽然原文也提到了检查胆固醇水平，但题目特别指明了是blood相关的检查，因此正确答案应该是"sugar"。此外，题目要求只填写一个单词，"sugar"完全符合这个要求。记住，在听力考试中，要特别注意题目的具体要求，并从听到的信息中准确提取所需的特定内容。

questions.title: · the library shop sells wall-charts, cards and 【   】
questions.analysis:
原因如下：
        1. 题目要求填写图书馆商店销售的第三种物品，前两种（wall-charts和cards）已经在题干中给出。
        2. 答案句中明确列举了商店销售的三种物品：wallcharts、greetings cards和stamps。
        3. "stamps"是句子中提到的第三种物品，正好符合题目要求。
        4. "stamps"与前面提到的邮寄卡片（"post the cards"）相呼应，进一步确认了它是一个独立的销售物品。
        5. 题目要求只填写一个单词，"stamps"正好符合这一要求。

        注意避免以下易错点：
        - 不要选择"greetings"，因为它只是"cards"的修饰词，不是独立的销售物品。
        - 不要选择"cards"，因为它已经在题干中给出，而且题目要求填写第三种物品。

questions.title: · evenings and weekends: free  【   】 is available
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写在晚上和周末免费的东西。在对话的最后部分，Paul询问是否有停车场，Susie回答说晚上和周末停车是免费的。这正好符合题目要求的条件：在晚上和周末免费的设施。因此，"parking"（停车）是正确答案。此外，这个答案也符合题目要求只填写一个单词的规定。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Hello?
transcript.cn: 苏西：你好？

transcript.en: PAUL: Hi, Susie, it's Paul here.
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗨，苏西，我是保罗。

transcript.en: How are you? Enjoying your new job?
transcript.cn: 最近怎么样？喜欢你的新工作吗？

transcript.en: You're working at the library, aren't you?
transcript.cn: 你是在图书馆工作，是吧？

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes. I started when the library re-opened a month ago. It's great.
transcript.cn: 苏西：是的。一个月前图书馆重新开馆时我开始在这工作的。图书馆很棒。

transcript.en: PAUL: Actually Carol and I have been meaning to join for a while.
transcript.cn: 保罗：实际上卡洛和我想来这已经有一段时间了。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Oh, you should. It doesn't cost anything, and the new library has all sorts of facilities.
transcript.cn: 苏西：嗯，你们应该来的。不需要花钱，而且新图书馆有各种各样的设施。

transcript.en: It's not Just a place where you borrow books.
transcript.cn: 它不仅仅是一个借书的地方。

transcript.en: For instance, there's an area with comfortable seats where you can sit and read the magazines they have there.
transcript.cn: 比如说，馆内有一片区域有很舒服的位子可以坐，还可以看那儿的杂志。

transcript.en: Some people spend the whole morning there.
transcript.cn: 有的人整个早上都在那。

transcript.en: PAUL: Mmm. Wish I had that amount of time to spend!
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗯。真希望我也有那么多时间。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes, you must be pretty busy at present, with the children and everything?
transcript.cn: 苏西：哦，你现在一定很忙吧，忙着照顾孩子什么的。

transcript.en: PAUL: We are, yes. But we're hoping to get away this summer.
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗯，是的。不过我们希望这个夏天能出去度假。

transcript.en: We're thinking of going to Greece.
transcript.cn: 我们正考虑去希腊。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Well, we've got a much larger section of the library devoted to travel books now, so you should come and have a look.
transcript.cn: 苏西：嗯，我们图书馆用于陈列旅游书籍的部分比以前大了很多，你可以过来看看。

transcript.en: I can't remember if there's anything specifically on Greece, but I should think so.
transcript.cn: 我不记得有没有专门讲希腊的书，但我想应该有。

transcript.en: PAUL: OK. Now Carol's organising a project for the history class she teaches at school - it's about life in the town a hundred years ago.
transcript.cn: 保罗：好的。卡洛目前正在为她在学校教的历史课组织一个活动——是关于100年前这个城市的生活的。

transcript.en: Do you have anything that might be useful?
transcript.cn: 你那有没有什么有用的资料？

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes, actually we've now got a new section with materials on the history of the town and surrounding region.
transcript.cn: 苏西：有的，事实上我们图书馆新开辟了一个部分，用于陈列这座城市及其周边区域的历史资料。

transcript.en: PAUL: Right. I'll tell her.
transcript.cn: 保罗：好的。我会告诉她的。

transcript.en: You can't always find that sort of thing on the internet.
transcript.cn: 你并一定总能在网上找到这些东西。

transcript.en: Now in the old library there used to be a separate room with reference books.
transcript.cn: 过去，在老图书馆里有一个单独的屋子存放参考书。

transcript.en: It was a really nice quiet room.
transcript.cn: 那真是一个又安静又好的地方。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes. We've put those books in the main part of the library now, but we do have a room called the community room.
transcript.cn: 苏西：是的。我们如今把那些书放在图书馆主馆，不过我们现在也有一个叫做社区用室的屋子。

transcript.en: It can be hired out for meetings, but at other times people can use it to study.
transcript.cn: 它可以被租出去用于开会，其他时候人们可以在里面学习。

transcript.en: PAUL: I might use that.
transcript.cn: 保罗：或许我会去那儿学习。

transcript.en: It's hard to find anywhere quiet at home sometimes.
transcript.cn: 有的时候想在家找到个安静的地方很难。

transcript.en: SUSIE: I can't remember how old your son and daughter are ... we've introduced a special section of fiction written specially for teenagers, but they might be a bit young for that?
transcript.cn: 苏西：我不记得你的儿子、女儿多大了……我们开辟了一个专为青少年写的小说的单元，但他们可能还是小了一点。

transcript.en: PAUL: Yes, they would be.
transcript.cn: 保罗：是的，他们还小。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Well, we do have lots of activities for younger children.
transcript.cn: 苏西：嗯，我们有很多针对年纪较小的孩子的活动。

transcript.en: PAUL: Yes?
transcript.cn: 保罗：是吗？

transcript.en: SUSIE: For example we have a Science Club.
transcript.cn: 苏西：比如说我们有一个“科学俱乐部”。

transcript.en: At the next meeting, they're going to be doing experiments with stuff that everyone has in the kitchen - sugar and flour and so on.
transcript.cn: 在俱乐部的下次会面上，孩子们会利用每家厨房里都有的东西——糖、面粉等做实验。

transcript.en: PAUL: They might be interested, yes.
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗯，我的孩子们也许会有兴趣。

transcript.en: SUSIE: And we have a competition for children called Reading Challenge.
transcript.cn: 苏西：我们还有一个比赛叫做“阅读挑战”。

transcript.en: That doesn 't begin until after the end of term.
transcript.cn: 这个比赛要到学期末过后才开始。

transcript.en: They have to read six books, and they get a certificate if they manage it.
transcript.cn: 他们要读完六本书，如果做到了的话就能得到一个证书。

transcript.en: PAUL: So that gives them something to do while they're on holiday, instead of getting bored.
transcript.cn: 保罗：这样他们假期就有事做而不会无聊了。

transcript.en: SUSIE: That's the idea.
transcript.cn: 苏西：嗯，就是这么想的。

transcript.en: And there's special activities for adults too.
transcript.cn: 图书馆也有针对成年人的特别活动。

transcript.en: On Friday we have a local author called Tanya Streep who's going to be talking about her new novel.
transcript.cn: 星期五，我们邀请了本地作家Tanya Streep来谈谈她的最新小说。

transcript.en: It's called 'Catch the Mouse' and she based the story on a crime that actually took place here years ago.
transcript.cn: 小说名叫《Catch the Mouse》，里面的故事是基于一个很多年前在这里发生的一起犯罪事件。

transcript.en: PAUL: Right. We're not free on Friday, but I'll look out for the book.
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗯，我们周五没空，不过我会找找这本小说。

transcript.en: SUSIE: Now this probably isn't for you, but we do have IT support available for members.
transcript.cn: 苏西：还有一个活动可能不适合你，针对会员我们推出了信息技术支持。

transcript.en: We get quite a few older people coming along who are wanting to get up to speed with computer technology.
transcript.cn: 有不少老人过来，想要跟上电脑技术的发展。

transcript.en: It's on Tuesday mornings - they don't need to make an appointment or anything, they just turn up.
transcript.cn: 这个活动是在星期二上午——他们不需要事先约定，到时候自然就来了。

transcript.en: PAUL: Well , my mother might be interested, I'll let her know.
transcript.cn: 保罗：嗯，我母亲或许会感兴趣，我会告诉她的。

transcript.en: SUSIE: OK. And there's another service which you wouldn't expect from a library, which is a free medical check-up.
transcript.cn: 苏西：好。还有一个活动你可能想不到图书馆会有，那就是免费的医疗检查。

transcript.en: The hospital arranges for someone to come along and measure the level of sugar in your blood, and they check cholesterol levels at the same time.
transcript.cn: 医院安排了人过来，可以测血糖，同时他们还会检测胆固醇水平。

transcript.en: PAUL: Really?
transcript.cn: 保罗：真的吗？

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes, but that's only for the over-60s, so you wouldn't qualify.
transcript.cn: 苏西：是的，不过这项服务仅提供给60岁以上的人，所以你不能参与。

transcript.en: PAUL: OK. Well, I'll tell my mother, she might be interested.
transcript.cn: 保罗：好的。我会告诉我妈，她也许会有兴趣。

transcript.en: SUSIE: What other information ... well , we do have a little shop with things like wallcharts and greetings cards, and also stamps so you can post the cards straightaway, which is really useful.
transcript.cn: 苏西：至于别的什么信息嘛……哦，我们还有一个小商店卖挂图、贺卡之类的东西，还有邮票，很有用，你可以在那直接把卡片寄出去。

transcript.en: PAUL: Yeah. Well, I'll bring the children round at the weekend and we'll join.
transcript.cn: 保罗：好的。周末我会带孩子们过来，我们要加入进来。

transcript.en: Oh, one more thing - I'll be bringing the car, is there parking available?
transcript.cn: 哦，还有一件事——我会开车过来，图书馆有停车的地方吗？

transcript.en: SUSIE: Yes, and it's free in the evening and at weekends.
transcript.cn: 苏西：有的，而且在晚上和周末免费。

transcript.en: PAUL: Perfect. Well, thanks, Susie see you soon …
transcript.cn: 保罗：完美。好的，谢谢你，苏西，再见……

questions.analysis: 这道题要求选出两个度假人数正在增长的年龄段。根据原文，65岁以上的年龄段是"increasing steadily year on year"（每年都在稳步增长），而55至64岁年龄段显示"noticeable growth"（明显增长）。因此，正确答案是D（55-64岁）和E（65岁以上）。其他年龄段要么是下降（16-30岁），要么是保持稳定（43-54岁），要么是没有增长迹象（31-42岁）。

questions.title: How does BC Travel plan to expand the painting holidays?

options.title: A
options.content: by adding to the number of locations

options.title: B
options.content: by increasing the range of levels

options.title: C
options.content: by employing more teachers

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C（by employing more teachers）。原文中明确提到"five additional ones will be joining us this year"，这里的"ones"指的是艺术家（artists），即教师。而雇佣更多教师的目的是"so that we can offer a greater number of classes in each centre"，即为了增加每个中心的课程数量。这直接对应了题目中问到的扩展计划。

questions.title: Why are BC Travel's cooking holidays unusual?

options.title: A
options.content: They only use organic foods.

options.title: B
options.content: They have an international focus.

options.title: C
options.content: They mainly involve vegetarian dishes.

questions.analysis: BC Travel的烹饪假期之所以不寻常，是因为它们关注来自多个不同国家的菜肴，而不是局限于一个特定国家。这种国际化的视角使得BC Travel的烹饪假期与其他旅行社提供的类似假期有所不同。答案B正确地反映了这一特点，指出这些假期具有国际焦点。

questions.title: What does the speaker say about the photography holidays?

options.title: A
options.content: Clients receive individual tuition.

options.title: B
options.content: The tutors are also trained guides.

options.title: C
options.content: Advice is given on selling photographs.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是A选项"Clients receive individual tuition"。原文中明确提到"clients can have one-on-one tuition during the holiday"，这里的"one-on-one tuition"就是"individual tuition"的同义表达。原文通过说明团队规模小（不超过8人）来解释为什么能够提供一对一的指导。这种个性化的教学方式是摄影假期的一个重要特点。

• wide variety of levels
• one holiday that is specially designed for $889$

questions.analysis: 题目要求填写在爱尔兰和意大利的健身假期除了个人设计的项目外还能减少什么。原文中明确提到，除了改善整体健康状况，客户还能减少日常生活中积累的压力（stress）。这正好符合题目中要求填写的内容。而且，"stress"是一个单词，符合题目要求只填写一个词的条件。此外，"stress"与健康和生活质量密切相关，在健身假期的背景下减少压力是一个合理且常见的益处。

题目要求填写希腊假期的主要目的。原文明确指出，在希腊提供的是一个两周的假期，专门针对那些"想要对他们的体重做些事情"的客户。这里的"do something about their weight"直接表明了这个假期的主要目的是控制体重。而且，后面还提到了个性化的饮食计划和在海滩上进行的运动课程，这些都进一步支持了"weight control"（体重控制）是这个假期的主要焦点。因此，"weight"是最合适的答案，它准确地概括了这个希腊假期的主要目的。

题目要求填写在摩洛哥特别设计的山地自行车度假类型。原文中明确提到了有一种度假方式是"tailored to the needs of families"（为家庭需求量身定制的），并且特别强调这种方式"particularly popular"（特别受欢迎）。这正好符合题目中要求的"specially designed for"（特别为...设计的）。

        此外，"families"作为答案也符合题目要求的ONE WORD ONLY（仅一个单词）。它既满足了语法要求，又准确地反映了原文中描述的特殊度假类型。

        在听力过程中，你需要特别注意"Morocco"和"mountain biking"这两个关键词出现后的信息。当听到"tailored to the needs of"这个表达时，应该意识到这可能就是题目所问的"specially designed for"的对应内容。

transcript.en: In this session in your training day we're going to look at some of the more specialised holidays we offer at BC Travel.
transcript.cn: 在培训日的这个部分，我们将会关注在BC旅行提供的专门的假期。

transcript.en: Now, the travel business is very competitive and it's important to be aware of how the market's changing and developing.
transcript.cn: 现在，旅游业务极具竞争力，因此重要的是要意识到市场的变化和发展。

transcript.en: In terms of age groups, the over-65s are an important market, and one that's increasing steadily year on year.
transcript.cn: 就年龄组而言，65岁以上是重要的市场。并且这一数字逐年稳步增长。

transcript.en: The fewest holidays are taken by the 31 to 42-year-olds, and that figure shows no sign of rising.
transcript.cn: 最少的假期是31至42岁，这个数字没有上升的迹象。

transcript.en: The biggest market at present is still the youngest group, the 16 to 30s, but this group's also seen the biggest drop over the last few years, whereas there's a noticeable growth in the number of holidays taken by the 55 to 64-year-olds.
transcript.cn: 目前最大的市场仍然是年龄最小的群体，即16至30岁，但该群体在过去几年中的跌幅也最大，而 55至64岁的人们度假的次数却有明显增长。

transcript.en: As far as the 43 to 54-year-olds are concerned, bookings there are steady, but I have to say we haven't seen the increase we expected.
transcript.cn: 就43至54岁的人而言，那里的预订量稳定，但我不得不说我们没有看到我们预期的增长。

transcript.en: One trend we're noticing with nearly all age groups is the growing popularity of holidays in which clients do some kind of specialised activity.
transcript.cn: 我们注意到几乎所有年龄段的一个趋势是，假期越来越流行，在假期中客户进行某种专门的活动。

transcript.en: I'm not talking here about adventure holidays, where clients take part in high-risk activities like white water rafting just for the thrill of it.
transcript.cn: 我不是在这里谈论冒险假期，在这个假期中，客户只是为了刺激而参加了高风险的活动，例如漂流。

transcript.en: Activity holidays usually involve rather less high-risk sports, or things like art and music.
transcript.cn: 活动假期通常涉及较少的高风险运动或诸如艺术和音乐之类的活动。

transcript.en: They're not necessarily cheaper than ordinary holidays, often the opposite, in fact.
transcript.cn: 实际上，它们不一定比普通假期便宜，通常相反。

transcript.en: But they do often take place outside the main tourist centres, which gives an opportunity for clients to find out more about the local people and customs, and many say this is one of the most positive features of these holidays.
transcript.cn: 但它们通常发生在主要旅游中心之外，这给客户提供了更多了解当地人和风俗的机会，许多人说这是这些假期最积极的特点之一。

transcript.en: Of course, they offer the chance to develop a new skill or talent, but clients often say that more than this, it's the chance to create lasting relationships with other like-minded people that's the main draw.
transcript.cn: 当然，他们提供了发展新技能或才能的机会，但客户通常会说的更多。这是与其他志趣相投的人建立持久关系的机会。

transcript.en: Let me give you some examples of BC Travel activity holidays.
transcript.cn: 让我给您一些BC Travel活动假期的例子。

transcript.en: Our painting holidays take place in four different centres in France and Italy and they're very popular with clients of all abilities from beginners onwards.
transcript.cn: 我们画的假期发生在法国和意大利四个不同的中心，他们是非常受欢迎的从初学者各种能力的客户开始。

transcript.en: We've got an excellent team of artists to lead the classes - some of them have been with us from the start and five additional ones will be joining us this year so that we can offer a greater number of classes in each centre.
transcript.cn: 我们拥有一支出色的艺术家团队来领导课程——其中有些人从一开始就与我们在一起。今年还将有另外五名艺术家加入我们，以便我们可以在每个中心提供更多班级。

transcript.en: As far as cooking holidays are concerned, I know a lot of agents offer holidays where clients cook recipes related to one particular country, usually the one they're staying in but we focus on dishes from a great many different ones.
transcript.cn: 烹饪假期而言，我知道有很多代理商提供假期，客户在这些假期中烹饪与一个特定国家/地区（通常是他们所居住的国家/地区）相关的食谱，但是我们会专注于来自于不同国家和地区的菜肴。

transcript.en: Apart from that you'll find the usual emphasis on good quality, organic ingredients - that's more or less a given nowadays - and there are generally some meat-free recipes included.
transcript.cn: 除此之外，您会发现通常都强调优质有机成分——如今或多或少是必需的——并且通常包括一些无肉食谱。

transcript.en: Our photography holidays take place in a wide range of countries from Iceland to Vietnam and clients have the opportunity to see some stunning scenery.
transcript.cn: 我们的摄影假期在从冰岛到越南的许多国家/地区举行，客户有机会看到一些绝妙的风景。

transcript.en: Groups are small, no more than eight, so clients can have one-on-one tuition during the holiday, and excursions are arranged with fully-trained guides.
transcript.cn: 团体人数不多，最多不超过8人，因此客户可以在假期享受一对一的辅导，而且游览还配有受过全面训练的向导。

transcript.en: At the end of each holiday an exhibition is held of the photographs taken so that clients can see one another's work and receive valuable feedback from the tutor.
transcript.cn: 在每个假期结束时，都会举办一次摄影展览，以便客户可以看到彼此的作品，并从导师那里获得宝贵的反馈。

transcript.en: Finally, let me tell you about our fitness holidays.
transcript.cn: 最后，让我告诉您我们的健身假期。

transcript.en: In Ireland and Italy we run one-week general fitness classes for all ages and levels of fitness.
transcript.cn: 在爱尔兰和意大利，我们针对所有年龄和水平的健身者开设为期一周的普通健身班。

transcript.en: Clients start the course with a consultation with a trainer, and together they draw up an individual programme.
transcript.cn: 客户在咨询培训师的基础上开始学习本课程，然后他们共同制定了单独的课程。

transcript.en: As well as improving general fitness, clients find that they end up losing much of the stress they've built up in their daily lives.
transcript.cn: 客户不仅提高了总体健康水平，还发现自己最终失去了在日常生活中积累的很多压力。

transcript.en: In Greece, we have a two-week holiday for clients who want to do something about their weight.
transcript.cn: 在希腊，我们为想要做些自己体重的客户提供两个星期的假期。

transcript.en: This has all the features you'd expect, like a personalised diet programme, but one of its most popular features is that the exercise classes are all held on the beach.
transcript.cn: 它具有您期望的所有功能，例如个性化的饮食计划，但最受欢迎的功能之一是所有的健身课程都在海滩上进行。

transcript.en: People say it's far preferable to being in a gym.
transcript.cn: 人们说这比在体育馆里好得多。

transcript.en: Finally, we offer several holidays in Morocco.
transcript.cn: 最后，我们在摩洛哥提供几个假期。

transcript.en: One very popular one is the mountain biking holiday.
transcript.cn: 一种非常受欢迎的是山地自行车假期。

transcript.en: Bikes are provided and there are different routes according to people's ability.
transcript.cn: 提供自行车，并且根据人们的能力提供不同的路线。

transcript.en: We offer one which is tailored to the needs of families, which is particularly popular.
transcript.cn: 我们提供一种特别适合家庭需求的产品。

transcript.en: OK, so that's about all the time I have today, so thank you very much ...
transcript.cn: 好的，这就是我今天所有的时间，非常感谢……

groups.desc:
Questions 21-26
Complete the flow-chart below.
Choose SIX answers from the box and write the correct letter, A-H, next to Questions 21-26.

questions.analysis: 第21题的正确答案是C（sources），因为听力原文中明确提到需要记录信息的来源，这与题干中的要求完全吻合。题干中的定位词是“Locate and read relevant articles”和“noting key information”，在听力原文中对应的信号词是“read articles”和“keep a record of”。听力原文中的答案句是“Did you remember to keep a record of where you got the information from?”，这句话的意思是“你记得记录你从哪里获得信息的吗？”，即信息来源（sources）。因此，正确答案是C。

解题思路：提到的第一种人是现场工作人员，故E。Dave 建议采访的第一类群体是 “The people who work there“（在那儿工作的人），这在语义上直接对应选项 E 中的 “staff“（员工）。这是案例研究中获取内部信息和运营现状最直接的渠道。避坑：录音中紧接着提到了 “tourists“（游客），但题目流程图中通常会有先后顺序。要准确捕捉第一个被提及的群体，并注意 “people who work there“ 与 “staff“ 的同义转换，不要因听到后面的干扰项而填错位置。

第23题的题干为“Select interviewees – these may be site ________ , visitors or city ________ 。”根据听力原文中的“we could even contact some people here in the city, like administrators involved in overseeing tourism.”，可以确定答案为“officials”（官员），因为“administrators involved in overseeing tourism”正是指城市里的官员。其他选项如“employees”指的是在场地工作的员工，不符合题干要求的“city”这个定位词。因此，正确答案是H（officials）。

第24题的问题是“Check whether 24 ............................... of interviewees can be used”。根据听力原文，“establish with our interviewees whether we can identify them in our case study or whether they want to be anonymous”，意思是确定是否能识别受访者或者他们是否希望匿名。这句话中的“identify them”就表明是否可以使用他们的名字。因此答案是“B. names”。其他选项的分析如下：
        
        - A. patterns：不相关，没有提到模式问题。
        - C. sources：不相关，没有提到资源问题。
        - D. questions：虽然有提到面试问题，但这里具体提到的是身份识别问题。
        - E. employees：题目只是“interviewees”，范围更广，不仅仅是员工。
        - F. solutions：没有提到解决方案的问题。
        - G. headings：不相关，没有提到标题问题。
        - H. officials：尽管提到了行政人员，但这里的重点是如何识别所有受访者。
第25题的正确答案是 "A patterns"。题干中的定位词是 "identify"，原文中对应的信号词是 "find out if we can identify"。答案句提到要找出任何趋势（trends）或规律（regularities），这些都与 "patterns" 的概念相符。答案句明确指出，在分析阶段，我们需要找出数据中的模式或趋势，因此 "A patterns" 是最合适的答案。

他们所做的案例研究不能以解决方案结尾，故F。Dave 强调了案例研究与普通报告的显著区别：普通报告通常以 “suggestions“（建议）结尾，而案例研究则是以向读者提出问题结尾，让他们决定 “what ought to be done“（该做什么）。这里的建议和对策均指向选项 F 中的 “solutions“（解决方案）。避坑：此题极具迷惑性，因为录音中提到案例研究结尾是 “question“。但由于选项中没有 “questions“，考生需要理解 “suggestions“ 是 “solutions“ 的近义词，并识别出讨论的重心在于如何处理发现的问题，从而选出最契合的抽象名词。

groups.title: The Horton Castle site
groups.desc:
Questions 27-30
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: Natalie and Dave agree one reason why so few people visit Horton Castle is that

options.title: A
options.content: the publicity is poor.

options.title: B
options.content: it is difficult to get to.

options.title: C
options.content: there is little there of interest.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是A "the publicity is poor"。Dave提到城堡网站的问题，说明宣传不佳是导致游客少的主要原因。虽然文中提到了交通问题，但那是引用了10年前的过时报告。Dave实际上说城堡有"plenty of fascinating stuff"，表明那里有很多有趣的东西。因此，通过分析最新的观点和排除过时信息，我们可以得出宣传不佳是最合理的答案。

questions.title: Natalie and Dave agree that the greatest problem with a visitor centre could be

options.title: A
options.content: covering the investment costs.

options.title: B
options.content: finding a big enough space for it.

options.title: C
options.content: dealing with planning restrictions.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是C（dealing with planning restrictions）。在讨论游客中心可能面临的问题时，说话者特别强调了由于城堡是历史遗址，建设游客中心需要获得特殊的规划许可，并且明确表示这可能会很困难。这个问题被突出强调，表明它被认为是最大的潜在障碍。其他提到的问题，如成本或空间，虽然也被提及，但没有被描述为同样困难或重要。

questions.title: What does Dave say about conditions in the town of Horton?

options.title: A
options.content: There is a lot of unemployment.

options.title: B
options.content: There are few people of working age.

options.title: C
options.content: There are opportunities for skilled workers.

questions.analysis: 正确答案是B，因为原文明确指出年轻人一旦获得技能或资格就会离开去城市工作，只剩下儿童和退休人员。这表明Horton镇缺少工作年龄的人口，符合选项B "There are few people of working age"（工作年龄的人很少）的描述。

questions.title: According to Natalie, one way to prevent damage to the castle site would be to

options.title: A
options.content: insist visitors have a guide.

options.title: B
options.content: make visitors keep to the paths.

options.title: C
options.content: limit visitor numbers.

questions.analysis: 这道题的正确答案是B选项（make visitors keep to the paths）。原文中提到，如果游客数量增加，可能需要实施一些限制措施，其中包括要求游客沿着标记的路径行走（"sticking to marked ways"）。这直接对应了选项B中的内容。这种措施旨在防止游客对城堡遗址造成潜在的损害，特别是在游客数量增加的情况下。通过限制游客的活动范围，可以更好地保护历史遗迹和环境。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Dave, I'm worried about our case study.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：戴夫, 我担心我们的案例研究。

transcript.en: I've done a bit of reading, but I'm not sure what's involved in actually writing a case study - I missed the lecture where Dr Baker talked us through it.
transcript.cn: 我读过一些文章，但我不清楚怎么写一篇案例研究——我好怀念贝克博士课上讲过的内容。

transcript.en: DAVE: OK, well it's quite straightforward.
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 好吧，其实很明了了。

transcript.en: We've got our focus - that's tourism at the Horton Castle site.
transcript.cn: 我们已经知道重点是什么了——就是关于霍顿城堡的旅游。

transcript.en: And you said you'd done some reading about it.
transcript.cn: 而且你说过你已经阅读了相关的一些资料。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Yes, I found some articles and made notes of the main points.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：是的，我找到了相关的一些文章，而且做了一些笔记。

transcript.en: DAVE: Did you remember to keep a record of where you got the information from?
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 你有记得记录你的信息出处吗？

transcript.en: NATALIE: Sure. I know what a pain it is when you forget that.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：当然了。我深知忘记记录出处的痛苦了。

transcript.en: DAVE: OK, so we can compare what we've read.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：好的。那我们就可以比较一下我们阅读过的文章了。

transcript.en: Then we have to decide on a particular problem or need at our site.
transcript.cn: 然后就可以决定这个景点的问题和需要所在。

transcript.en: And then think about who we're going to interview to get more information.
transcript.cn: 最后决定由谁去采访更多的信息。

transcript.en: NATALIE: OK. So who'd that be?
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：好的。那是谁呢？

transcript.en: The people who work there?
transcript.cn: 在那里上班的人？

transcript.en: And presumably some of the tourists too?
transcript.cn: 还是游客？

transcript.en: DAVE: Yes, both those groups.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：是的，两组都是。

transcript.en: So we'll have to go to the site to do that, I suppose.
transcript.cn: 所以我猜想我们要到那里去。

transcript.en: But we might also do some of our interviewing away from the site - we could even contact some people here in the city, like administrators involved in overseeing tourism.
transcript.cn: 但我们可能也需要在其他地方进行采访——我们甚至还可以联系这个城市里的人，比如负责旅游业的官员。

transcript.en: NATALIE: OK. So we'll need to think about our interview questions and fix times and places for the meetings.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：好的。那我们就需要思考一下采访的问题以及会面的时间和地点。

transcript.en: It's all going to take a lot of time.
transcript.cn: 这都需要花费很长时间

transcript.en: DAVE: Mmm. And if we can, we should ask our interviewees if they can bring along some numerical data that we can add to support our findings.
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 嗯。并且如果可以的话，我们可以问问受访者是否可以带一些数据来，这样我们就可以把它添加进报告中了。

transcript.en: NATALIE: And photographs?
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：照片呢？

transcript.en: DAVE: I think we have plenty of those already.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：我觉得照片已经足够了。

transcript.en: But Dr Baker also said we have to establish with our interviewees whether we can identify them in our case study or whether they want to be anonymous.
transcript.cn: 但是贝克博士也提到我们必须和受访者沟通好，关于他们是否愿意在案例研究中出现，还是说希望以匿名的形式。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Oh, I wouldn't have thought of that.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：哦，这个我倒没想到。

transcript.en: OK, once we've got all this information, I suppose we have to analyse it.
transcript.cn: 一旦我们得到了信息，我想我们就要去分析一下了。

transcript.en: DAVE: Yes, put it all together and choose what's relevant to the problem we're focusing on, and analyse that carefully to find out if we can identify any trends or regularities there.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：是的，把所有信息都放到一起，然后选出和我们研究问题相关的信息，并且仔细分析出我们是否可以找到任何的趋势和规律。

transcript.en: That's the main thing at this stage, rather than concentrating on details or lots of facts.
transcript.cn: 这才是这个阶段的主要事情，并非关注一些细节和事实。

transcript.en: NATALIE: OK. And then once we've analysed that, what next?
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：好的。那我们分析之后，接下来干什么？

transcript.en: DAVE: Well, then we need to think about what we do with the data we've selected to make it as clear as possible to our readers.
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 然后我们要考虑如何让我们选择的数据让读者尽可能的看的清楚。

transcript.en: Things like graphs, or tables, or charts …
transcript.cn: 比如说用一些图表，表格等等……

transcript.en: NATALIE: Right.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：对的。

transcript.en: DAVE: Then the case study itself is mostly quite standard; we begin by presenting the problem, and giving some background, then go through the main sections, but the thing that surprised me is that in a normal report we'd end with some suggestions to deal with the problem or need we identified, but in a case study we end up with a question or a series of questions to our readers and they decide what ought to be done.
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 这个案例研究本身是非常标准化的。我们通过提出问题开始，然后给一些背景知识，接着来到主要部分，但让我感到惊讶的是在寻常的报告中，都是以提出解决问题的建议来结束，但是在案例研究中，我们以向大家提出问题结束，然后由他们来决定应该做什么。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Oh I hadn't realised that.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：这个我还没意识到。

transcript.en: NATALIE: So basically, the problem we're addressing in our case study of the Horton Castle site is why so few tourists are visiting it.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：因此，从本质上来说，我们需要在案例研究中着重关注的是为什么这么少的游客来霍顿城堡。

transcript.en: And we'll find out more from our interviews, but I did find one report on the internet that suggested that one reason might be because as far as transport goes, access is difficult.
transcript.cn: 而且我们应该更多的从受访者中找到问题。但是我看到一篇网上的报告，提到主要的一个原因就是交通不够方便。

transcript.en: DAVE: I read that too, but that report was actually written ten years ago, when the road there was really bad, but that's been improved now.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：我也读过这篇报告，但这是十年前写的，当时路况非常差，但现在已经修好了。

transcript.en: And I think there's plenty of fascinating stuff there for a really good day out, but you'd never realise it from the castle website - maybe that's the problem.
transcript.cn: 并且我认为那里有许多迷人的东西值得游玩，从网站上压根就看不出什么东西——也许这才是问题所在。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Yes, it's really dry and boring.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：是的，特别枯燥乏味。

transcript.en: DAVE: I read somewhere a suggestion that what the castle needs is a visitor centre.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：我看过一个提议说这个城堡需要的是一个游客中心。

transcript.en: So we could have a look for some information about that on the internet.
transcript.cn: 这样我们就可以在网上关注一些信息。

transcript.en: What would we need to know?
transcript.cn: 我们需要了解什么呢？

transcript.en: NATALIE: Well, who'd use it for a start.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：先从谁使用它开始。

transcript.en: It'd be good to know what categories the visitors fell into too, like school parties or retired people, but I think we'd have to talk to staff to get that information .
transcript.cn: 这样就可以了解参观者的类别，比如是学校人员还是退休人员。但是我想我们需要和员工交流一下才可以知道这些信息。

transcript.en: DAVE: OK. And as we're thinking of suggesting a visitor centre we'd also have to look at potential problems.
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 好的。并且由于我么在考虑建议建一个游客中心，我们还需要考虑到潜在的问题。

transcript.en: I mean, obviously it wouldn't be cheap to set up.
transcript.cn: 我的意思是，建一个游客中心肯定不便宜。

transcript.en: NATALIE: No, but it could be a really good investment.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：是的，但这一定是一个有价值的投资。

transcript.en: And as it's on a historical site it'd need to get special planning permission, I expect.
transcript.cn: 因为这是个历史遗迹，这也需要得到规划许可，我猜想。

transcript.en: That might be hard.
transcript.cn: 这个可能会很困难。

transcript.en: DAVE: Right, especially as the only possible place for it would be at the entrance, and that's right in front of the castle.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：对的，特别是唯一可能的地点是入口处，就在城堡前面。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Mmm.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：是啊。

transcript.en: DAVE: But it could be a good thing for the town of Horton.
transcript.cn: 戴夫：但这对霍顿镇来说是件好事。

transcript.en: At present it's a bit of a ghost town.
transcript.cn: 现在它有点像废弃的小镇。

transcript.en: Once they've left school and got any skills or qualifications, the young people all get out as fast as they can to get jobs in the city, and the only people left are children and those who've retired.
transcript.cn: 年轻人一旦毕业后或者获得了一些技能或证书资格，他们就立马到城市里去找工作，剩下的就只有小孩和那些退休的人。

transcript.en: NATALIE: Right. Something else we could investigate would be the potential damage that tourists might cause to the castle site, I mean their environmental impact.
transcript.cn: 娜塔莉：对的。我们还要调查的是游客对城堡的潜在伤害，我的意思是他们带来的环境影响。

transcript.en: At present the tourists can just wander round wherever they want, but if numbers increase, there might have to be some restrictions, like sticking to marked ways.
transcript.cn: 现在游客可以去到任何他想去的地方游玩，但是如果游客数量增加，这里就会有许多限制，比如说要遵循规定的路线。

transcript.en: And there'd need to be guides and wardens around to make sure these were enforced.
transcript.cn: 那就需要导游和看守人员来确保这些。

transcript.en: DAVE: Yes, we could look at that too. OK, well …
transcript.cn: 戴夫： 是的，我们也要关注一下这个问题。好……

groups.title: The effects of environmental change on birds
groups.desc:
Questions 31-40
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.collect: Mercury (Hg) • Highly toxic • Released into the atmosphere from coal • In water it may be consumed by fish • It has also recently been found to affect birds which feed on $896$ Research on effects of mercury on birds • Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:               - the effects on birds' $897$ or mental processes, e.g. memory               - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's $898$ ) • Findings:               - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less $899$               - this may have a negative effect on birds' $900$ • Lab-based studies:               - allow more $901$ for the experimenter Implications for humans • Migrating birds such as $902$ containing mercury may be eaten by humans • Mercury also causes problems in learning $903$ • Mercury in a mother's body from $904$ may affect the unborn child • New regulations for mercury emissions will affect everyone's energy $905$

questions.title: · It has also recently been found to affect birds which feed on 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写最近发现会影响的鸟类所吃的食物。原文中明确指出，长期以来人们知道吃鱼的鸟类会受到影响，但直到最近才发现吃昆虫的鸟类也会受到影响。因此，"insects"（昆虫）是正确答案。这个答案直接对应了题干中的"recently found"（最近发现），并且与原文中的对比结构相符合，突出了这是一个新的发现。此外，文中使用了"what wasn't known until quite recently"这一表达，进一步强调了这是最近才知道的信息，与题干要求完全吻合。

questions.title: · Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:   - the effects on birds'   【   】 or mental processes, e.g. memory    - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's  【   】 ) · Findings:   - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less  【   】    - this may have a negative effect on birds'  【   】 · Lab-based studies:   - allow more  【   】 for the experimenter
questions.analysis:
：

1. 题目要求填写一个词，描述Claire Varian-Ramos正在研究的鸟类效应。

2. 原文中提到她正在研究"more subtle sub-effects"，这些效应可能与鸟类的"behaviour"（行为）或"the way their brain works"（大脑工作方式）有关。

3. "behaviour"是一个更广泛的概念，涵盖了鸟类的各种行为表现，包括但不限于记忆问题。

4. 虽然原文也提到了"memory"（记忆），但这只是作为一个例子被提及，而不是全面描述研究内容的词。

5. "behaviour"更好地概括了研究的整体范围，包括可能影响鸟类行为的各种因素。

6. 此外，"behaviour"与题干中提到的"mental processes"（心理过程）相对应，因为行为往往是心理过程的外在表现。

因此，"behaviour"（或美式英语拼写"behavior"）是最合适的答案，既符合原文内容，又能全面概括研究的重点。

questions.title: · Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:   - the effects on birds'   【   】 or mental processes, e.g. memory    - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's  【   】 ) · Findings:   - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less  【   】    - this may have a negative effect on birds'  【   】 · Lab-based studies:   - allow more  【   】 for the experimenter
questions.analysis: 原文明确指出幼鸟是通过聆听其父亲的歌声来学习唱歌的。这个信息直接回答了题干中关于鸟类歌唱学习来源的问题。原文使用了比较结构"rather than by any other bird"，进一步强调了父鸟在这个学习过程中的独特作用。这个细节非常重要，因为它排除了其他可能的选项（如母鸟或其他成年鸟）。此外，这个信息与题干中提到的"usually learned from a bird's [blank]"完全吻合，使"father"成为最合适的答案。

questions.title: · Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:   - the effects on birds'   【   】 or mental processes, e.g. memory    - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's  【   】 ) · Findings:   - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less  【   】    - this may have a negative effect on birds'  【   】 · Lab-based studies:   - allow more  【   】 for the experimenter
questions.analysis: 原文中直接比较了暴露于汞的鸟类和其他鸟类所产生的歌声。关键词是"aren't as complex as"，这清楚地表明了暴露于汞的鸟类产生的歌声不如其他鸟类的复杂。"Complex"是用来描述歌声差异的准确词汇，它符合题目要求的一个词的填空，并且直接回答了题干中关于汞暴露对鸟类歌声影响的问题。此外，这个答案也与科学研究的语境相符，因为复杂性是衡量鸟类歌声的一个重要特征。

questions.title: · Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:   - the effects on birds'   【   】 or mental processes, e.g. memory    - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's  【   】 ) · Findings:   - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less  【   】    - this may have a negative effect on birds'  【   】 · Lab-based studies:   - allow more  【   】 for the experimenter
questions.analysis: 原文中直接提到了"reproduction"这个词。文章讨论了汞对鸟类的影响，特别是对它们的繁殖能力的影响。原文指出，暴露于汞的雄鸟可能会变得对雌鸟不那么有吸引力，这直接影响了它们的繁殖机会。"reproduction"（繁殖）是这个影响链的最终结果，体现了汞污染对鸟类种群延续的负面影响。这个词准确地概括了讨论的核心问题，即汞污染如何影响鸟类的繁衍能力。选择"reproduction"作为答案不仅直接对应原文，还准确地反映了科学研究关注的关键点。

questions.title: · Claire Varian-Ramos is investigating:   - the effects on birds'   【   】 or mental processes, e.g. memory    - the effects on bird song (usually learned from a bird's  【   】 ) · Findings:   - songs learned by birds exposed to mercury are less  【   】    - this may have a negative effect on birds'  【   】 · Lab-based studies:   - allow more  【   】 for the experimenter
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写实验室研究允许实验者拥有更多的什么。原文中明确指出，实验室研究的优势在于实验者"has a much higher level of control"（有更高水平的控制）。"control"这个词直接对应了题目中所问的内容，表示实验者在实验室环境中能够更好地控制各种变量，从而获得更可靠的研究结果。此外，"control"是一个常见的科学研究术语，特别是在讨论实验方法时经常使用，因此它是最合适的答案。其他可能出现的词如"variables"或"confidence"虽然也在相关句子中出现，但它们并不直接回答题目所问的"实验者拥有更多的什么"这个问题。

questions.title: · Migrating birds such as  【   】 containing mercury may be eaten by humans
questions.analysis:
原因如下：

1. 题目要求填写一种可能被人类食用的迁徙鸟类。
2. 原文中明确提到"ducks"（鸭子）作为一个具体例子，说明鸭子是迁徙鸟类。
3. 文中提到这些鸭子在受污染的地方觅食，后来被猎人在很远的地方猎杀，这体现了鸭子的迁徙特性。
4. 原文使用"presumably eaten"（很可能被食用）直接表明这些鸭子可能被人类食用。
5. 这个例子完全符合题目要求的"含汞的迁徙鸟类可能被人类食用"这一情况。

因此，"ducks"（鸭子）是最符合题目要求的答案。它既是迁徙鸟类，又可能含有汞（因为在受污染地区觅食），还可能被人类食用。这个例子精确地说明了题目所描述的情况。

questions.title: · Mercury also causes problems in learning 【   】
questions.analysis: 题目要求填写mercury在人类学习方面造成的问题。原文中明确指出，在人类中，汞会导致语言习得（language acquisition）的发展延迟。"Language"（语言）是人类特有的学习内容，与题目要求完全吻合。此外，"acquisition"（习得）是"learning"（学习）的同义词，进一步确认了答案的正确性。虽然文章前面讨论了汞对鸟类歌唱学习的影响，但题目特别指明是关于人类的影响，所以正确答案必须与人类相关。因此，"language"是最适合的答案。

questions.title: · Mercury in a mother's body from  【   】 may affect the unborn child
questions.analysis: 原文明确指出汞通过母亲食用的食物影响未出生的孩子。题目要求填写进入母亲体内的物质来源，而不是汞本身。原文中提到"the food eaten by its mother contains high levels of mercury"，明确指出食物是汞进入母体的途径。因此，"food"是最准确的答案，它直接回答了题目中询问的汞如何进入母亲体内并影响未出生孩子的问题。

questions.title: · New regulations for mercury emissions will affect everyone's energy 【   】
questions.answer: costs / cost / price / bill / bills / prices
questions.analysis: 原文明确提到新的汞排放规定会"increase costs for everyone"（增加每个人的成本）。这直接对应了题干中提到的"will affect everyone's energy"，而"costs"正是填空的正确答案。这里的"costs"指的是能源成本，与题干中的"energy"相关联。此外，题目要求填写一个单词，"costs"正好符合这一要求。原文通过提到"billions of dollars"和"increase costs"强调了这些新规定的经济影响，进一步支持了"costs"作为正确答案的合理性。

transcript.en: OK, so we've been looking at how man-made changes in our environment can affect wildlife.
transcript.cn: 好了，我们已经看到环境中人类的变化是如何影响野生动物的了。

transcript.en: Now I'll discuss a particular example.
transcript.cn: 现在我要讨论一个具体的案例。

transcript.en: Let's take a look at mercury.
transcript.cn: 让我们来看一下汞元素。

transcript.en: Mercury's one of the 120 or so elements that make up all matter, and it has the symbol Hg.
transcript.cn: 汞是我们所需的120种元素之一,而且它的符号是Hg。

transcript.en: It's a shiny, silvery substance.
transcript.cn: 它是种发光的，银色的物质。

transcript.en: You may have seen it in old-fashioned thermometers, but it's not used much for domestic purposes now because it's highly toxic.
transcript.cn: 你可能在老式温度计上看到过它，但它现在已经不大作为家庭用途了，因为它有剧毒。

transcript.en: But the problem is that the amount of mercury in the environment's increasing.
transcript.cn: 但问题是环境中的汞含量不断增加。

transcript.en: The main reason for this is the power plants used to produce electricity.
transcript.cn: 主要原因就是发电站用来发电。

transcript.en: The main source of energy that most of them use is still coal, and when it's burned it releases mercury into the atmosphere.
transcript.cn: 发电站使用的能量来源主要是煤炭，当煤炭燃烧时就会释放出汞，进入到空气中。

transcript.en: Some of this gets deposited into lakes and rivers, and if it's ingested by a fish it's not excreted, it stays in the fish's body and it enters the food chain.
transcript.cn: 一些汞进入到湖里和河里，并且被鱼食用，导致无法从体内排出，因此进入到食物链中。

transcript.en: So it's been known for some time that birds which eat fish may be affected, but what wasn't known until quite recently is that those that eat insects can also be affected.
transcript.cn: 因此我们了解到一些鸟吃了这些鱼之后就会被影响，但我们最近才知道的是那些吃了昆虫的鸟也会被影响到。

transcript.en: So a woman called Claire Varian-Ramos is doing some research on how this is affecting birds.
transcript.cn: 有一个叫克莱尔·拉莫斯瓦里安的女性正在进行一项关于这会如何影响到鸟类的研究。

transcript.en: And rather than looking at how many birds are actually killed by mercury poisoning, she's looking for more subtle sub-effects .
transcript.cn: 她没有把重点放在有多少鸟死于汞中毒，而是把注意力放在更细微的副作用上。

transcript.en: And these may be to do with the behaviour of the birds, or with the effect of mercury on the way their brain works, so whether it leads to problems with memory, for example.
transcript.cn: 这个可能和鸟的行为有关，或者和汞对大脑运行的影响有关，比如它是否对记忆问题有影响。

transcript.en: And she's particularly focusing on the effects of mercury on bird song.
transcript.cn: 她尤其关注汞对鸟鸣的影响。

transcript.en: Now, the process of song learning happens at a particular stage in the birds' development, and what you may not know is that a young bird seems to acquire this skill by listening to the songs produced by its father, rather than by any other bird.
transcript.cn: 鸟类学习鸣叫的过程发生在特别的成长阶段，并且你可能不知道的是一只幼鸟是通过听它父亲鸣叫来习得这种技能的，而不是通过其他鸟类。

transcript.en: And Varian-Ramos has already found in her research that if young male birds are exposed to mercury, if they eat food contaminated with mercury, then the songs they produce aren't as complex as those produced by other birds.
transcript.cn: 拉莫斯瓦里安已经在她的研究中发现如果年轻的雄鸟暴露在汞的环境中，如果它们食用了含有汞的食物，那他们的鸣叫就没有其他鸟那么多样。

transcript.en: So quite low-level exposure to mercury is likely to have an impact on male birds in a natural situation, because it can mean that they're less attractive to female birds, and so it can affect their chances of reproduction.
transcript.cn: 因此，在自然环境中，接触少量汞很可能对雄鸟有一定的影响，因为这意味着它们对雌鸟的吸引力下降，继而影响它们繁殖的机会。

transcript.en: Now the way she's carrying out this research is worth thinking about.
transcript.cn: 现在，她开展研究的方式也值得思考。

transcript.en: She's using a mixture of studies using birds kept in laboratories, and studies carried out outdoors in the wild.
transcript.cn: 她运用了混合研究，既使用了实验室里的鸟进行研究，也在户外野生环境下开展研究。

transcript.en: The lab-based studies have the advantage that you don't get all the variables you would in a natural setting, so the experimenter has a much higher level of control, and that means they can be more confident about their results in some ways.
transcript.cn: 实验室研究的好处是，它没有在自然背景下研究会存在的诸多变量，因此，实验能够在更加可控的状况下进行，这也意味着在某种程度上他们对研究成果更加自信。

transcript.en: And of course they don't have to worry about going out and finding the birds in order to observe them.
transcript.cn: 当然了，他们也不用担心要去到户外找到一些鸟类来观察。

transcript.en: So what are the implications here for humans?
transcript.cn: 那这对人类的暗示是什么呢？

transcript.en: Well, because many birds are migratory, they may be transporting mercury far from contaminated sites.
transcript.cn: 由于许多鸟都是迁徙而来的，他们可能会从污染地携带汞而来。

transcript.en: For example, it's been found that ducks who'd been feeding at a contaminated site were later shot by hunters over a thousand kilometres away, and presumably eaten.
transcript.cn: 比如说，在污染地喂食的鸭之后被千米以外的猎人猎杀并很可能被吃掉。

transcript.en: But these birds likely had mercury levels high enough to warrant concern for human consumption.
transcript.cn: 但是这些很可能含汞超量的鸟足以对人类消费造成威胁。

transcript.en: In addition, going back to song learning by birds, we saw that this may be affected by mercury contamination.
transcript.cn: 除此之外，回到刚才说的鸟鸣的习得，我们看到这个可能会被汞污染所影响。

transcript.en: Well, we also know that in humans, mercury causes developmental delays in the acquisition of language, and in fact this process is very similar in the brain regions it involves and even the genes that are involved.
transcript.cn: 我们也知道对人类来说，汞会造成语言习得的延缓，事实上，这个过程在所涉及到的人脑区域甚至基因都非常相似。

transcript.en: But mercury contamination has other important implications for humans as well.
transcript.cn: 但是汞污染也对人类有其他重要的启发。

transcript.en: It's now known that an unborn child can be affected if the food eaten by its mother contains high levels of mercury, and these effects can be quite substantial.
transcript.cn: 现在我们知道，如果妈妈食用了富含汞的食物，未出世的小孩会受到极大影响，而这些影响是异常深远的。

transcript.en: In the end, it comes down to whether more value is placed on human economic wellbeing or environmental wellbeing.
transcript.cn: 最后，又回到了应该侧重经济发展还是环境发展的问题。

transcript.en: It's true there are new regulations for mercury emissions from power plants, but these will need billions of dollars to implement, and increase costs for everyone.
transcript.cn: 确实有新的规定制约发电厂的汞排放问题，但是这需要数十亿美元的支持，也加大了成本。

transcript.en: Some argue that's too much to pay to protect wild life.
transcript.cn: 有人认为为了保护环境付出的代价太大了。

transcript.en: But as we've seen, the issues go beyond that, and I think it's an issue we need to consider very carefully.
transcript.cn: 但我们可以看到，问题远远超出了这些，并且我认为这是我们必须认真思考的问题。
